Crash/debug-path compatibility fix whose PR text says the ticket required no new tests, but test files changed.

requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: Linked issue scikit-learn/scikit-learn#33034: `KNeighborsClassifier` fails on non-numeric labels with `ValueError` on certain parameters combination
### Describe the bug
When used with `algorithm="brute"` (or `algorithm="auto"` and the heuristic suggests brute force) and `p = 1`, `KNeighborsClassifier.predict` crashes when y lab
Note that this only crashes for this specific param combination, and `p=2`, for example, works fine with non-integer labels
This happens because `self.classes_` is passed to `unique_Y_labels` here:
And the [docs](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/66200f149ba6c64a3a93dd73e37bdeea87bb5db8/sklearn/metrics/_pairwise_distances_reduction/_dispatc
while `self.classes_` contains actual values, not indices
### Steps/Code to Reproduce

If I manually set the position of an Axes, then call twinx or twiny, the new twinned Axes appears in the original position of the parent Axes, not in the new po
![MPL_Bug_Image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/51108483/138197926-c1dc8d1b-e36c-4089-a1dd-073bf6c61ca7.png)

I also note that the same issue occurs for `ax.twiny()`

after calling `ax2=ax.twinx()`

requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: ### Motivation / Background
`ActiveSupport::NumberHelper.number_to_rounded` already formats infinities and NaN as `"Inf"`, `"-Inf"`, `"NaN"`:
This is the explicit `rounded_number.finite?` branch in `NumberToRoundedConverter#convert` that falls back to `"%f" % rounded_number`
Once `significant: true` is added, however, the same input raises `FloatDomainError`:
The crash propagates to every helper that routes through `RoundingHelper` with the same options — `number_to_percentage`, `number_to_currency`, `number_to_delim
The default options for `number_to_human` and `number_to_human_size` use `significant: true` internally too, but those helpers have additional `log10` / `log` c
The bug shows up most often when a calculation can produce `Infinity` — division by zero on a Float, an overflowed `**`, an imported value with a literal `"Infi
The crash is on this line in `RoundingHelper#digit_count`:

requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: Linked issue go-gitea/gitea#38171: OIDC auto account linking fails with HTTP 500 when the existing Gitea user has local 2FA/WebAuthn enabled
### Gitea Version
### What happened?
Hey there, I encountered the following bug:
When `[oauth2_client].ACCOUNT_LINKING = auto` is enabled and an OIDC user logs in whose email/username matches an existing local Gitea account, Gitea enters the
If that existing account has local Gitea 2FA/WebAuthn configured, Gitea redirects to the 2FA flow
After entering a valid TOTP code, the login fails with HTTP
The relevant log line is:

requirementSignalSummary.requirementText: #### Trac ticket number
#### Branch description
This PR fixes the issue described in ticket #36112, where ``DatabaseOperations.last_executed_query()`` in the Oracle backend may return ``None`` when a query fa
This causes SQL formatting to crash under ``--debug-sql``
Following the core team’s guidance on the ticket (Jacob Walls & Mariusz Felisiak), backend implementations should fall back to ``super().last_executed_query()``
Adds the fallback for Oracle
Matches the expected backend contract
Has no behavioral impact on successful queries
